case_title: PAUL O'GRADY VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al

docket_entries.description: NOTICE OF ENTRY OF ORDER/NOTICE OF RULING FILED SUSTAININ DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT FILED BY DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO

OPPOSITION TO DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT, POINTS AND AUTHORITIES FILED BY PLAINTIFF O'GRADY PLUMBING
MINI MINUTES FOR JAN-18-2012 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING: SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFF'S MONETARY CLAIMS ARE BARRED BY THE SIX MONTHS LIMITATIONS FOR GOVERNMENT CLAIMS ACT CLAIMS. PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST FOR INJUNCTIVE RELIEF IS DEFICIENT SINCE PLAINTIFF DOES NOT ALLEGE, OR ANY SUGGESTION THAT IT COULD ALLEGE, THAT THERE IS ANY REASONABLE CHANCE THAT IT WILL BE REQUIRED TO PAY THE ALLEGEDLY "UNLAWFUL PAYMENT" IN THE FUTURE OR, EVEN IF IT WOULD BE REQUIRED TO PAY THE FEE AGAIN, THAT A FUTURE REFUND LAWSUIT IS AN INADEQUATE REMEDY. (5 WITKIN, CALIFORNIA PROCEDURE (5TH ED.) "PLEADING" SECTIONS 823 AND 826). ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, REPORTER: TEANNA WARD, CSR # 11918

calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Feb-10-2012 Off Calendar

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-18-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. FREDERICK P. SHEINFIELD, TEL. NO. 554-3872 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING: DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS Sustained without leave to amend. Plaintiff's monetary claims are barred by the six months limitations for Government Claims Act claims. Plaintiff's request for injunctive relief is deficient since plaintiff does not allege, or any suggestion that it could allege, that there is any reasonable chance that it will be required to pay the allegedly "unlawful payment" in the future or, even if it would be required to pay the fee again, that a future refund lawsuit is an inadequate remedy. (5 Witkin, California Procedure (5th ed.) "Pleading" sections 823 and 826). ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: Teanna Ward, CSR # 11918 =(302/HEK)
